info.name: Numbers
info.desc: The Fourth Book of Moses called Numbers

1:
On the first day of the second month in the second year after the people of Israel left Egypt, the 
LORD
 spoke to Moses there in the Tent of his presence in the Sinai Desert. He said,
2: “You and Aaron are to take a census of the people of Israel by clans and families. List the names of all the men
The First Census of Israel
3: twenty years old or older who are fit for military service.
The First Census of Israel
4: Ask one clan chief from each tribe to help you.”
The First Census of Israel
5-16:
These are the men, leaders within their tribes, who were chosen from the community for this work:
TribeClan chief
ReubenElizur son of Shedeur
SimeonShelumiel son of Zurishaddai
JudahNahshon son of Amminadab
IssacharNethanel son of Zuar
ZebulunEliab son of Helon
EphraimElishama son of Ammihud
ManassehGamaliel son of Pedahzur
BenjaminAbidan son of Gideoni
DanAhiezer son of Ammishaddai
AsherPagiel son of Ochran
GadEliasaph son of Deuel
NaphtaliAhira son of Enan
17: With the help of these twelve men Moses and Aaron
The First Census of Israel
18: called together the whole community on the first day of the second month and registered all the people by clans and families. The names of all the men twenty years old or older were recorded and counted,
The First Census of Israel
19:
as the 
LORD
 had commanded. In the Sinai Desert, Moses registered the people.
The First Census of Israel
20-46:
The men twenty years old or older who were fit for military service were registered by name according to clan and family, beginning with the tribe of Reuben, Jacob's eldest son. The totals were as follows:
TribeNumber
Reuben46,500
Simeon59,300
Gad45,650
Judah74,600
Issachar54,400
Zebulun57,400
Ephraim40,500
Manasseh32,200
Benjamin35,400
Dan62,700
Asher41,500
Naphtali53,400
Total:603,550
47: The Levites were not registered with the other tribes,
48:
because the 
LORD
 had said to Moses,
49: “When you take a census of the men fit for military service, do not include the tribe of Levi.
50: Instead, put the Levites in charge of the Tent of my presence and all its equipment. They shall carry it and its equipment, serve in it, and set up their camp round it.
51: Whenever you move your camp, the Levites shall take the Tent down and set it up again at each new camping place. Anyone else who comes near the Tent shall be put to death.
52: The rest of the Israelites shall set up camp, company by company, each man with his own group and under his own banner.
53: But the Levites shall camp round the Tent to guard it, so that no one may come near and cause my anger to strike the community of Israel.”
54:
So the people of Israel did everything that the 
LORD
 had commanded Moses.

1:
The 
LORD
 gave Moses and Aaron the following instructions.
The Arrangement of the Tribes in Camp
2: When the Israelites set up camp, each man will camp under the banner of his division and the flag of his own clan. The camp is to be set up all round the Tent.
The Arrangement of the Tribes in Camp
3-9:
On the east side, those under the banner of the division of Judah shall camp in their groups, under their leaders, as follows:
TribeLeaderNumber
JudahNahshon son of Amminadab74,600
IssacharNethanel son of Zuar54,400
ZebulunEliab son of Helon57,400
Total:186,400
The division of Judah shall march first.
The Arrangement of the Tribes in Camp
10-16:
On the south, those under the banner of the division of Reuben shall camp in their groups, under their leaders, as follows:
TribeLeaderNumber
ReubenElizur son of Shedeur46,500
SimeonShelumiel son of Zurishaddai59,300
GadEliasaph son of Deuel45,650
Total:151,450
The division of Reuben shall march second.
The Arrangement of the Tribes in Camp
17: Then, between the first two divisions and the last two the Levites are to march carrying the Tent. Each division shall march in the same order as they camp, each in position under its banner.
The Arrangement of the Tribes in Camp
18-24:
On the west, those under the banner of the division of Ephraim shall camp in their groups, under their leaders, as follows:
TribeLeaderNumber
EphraimElishama son of Ammihud40,500
ManassehGamaliel son of Pedahzur32,200
BenjaminAbidan son of Gideoni35,400
Total:108,100
The division of Ephraim shall march third.
The Arrangement of the Tribes in Camp
25-31:
On the north, those under the banner of the division of Dan shall camp in their groups, under their leaders, as follows:
TribeLeaderNumber
DanAhiezer son of Ammishaddai62,700
AsherPagiel son of Ochran41,500
NaphtaliAhira son of Enan53,400
Total:157,600
The division of Dan shall march last.
The Arrangement of the Tribes in Camp
32: The total number of the people of Israel enrolled in the divisions, group by group, was 603,550.
The Arrangement of the Tribes in Camp
33:
As the 
LORD
 had commanded Moses, the Levites were not registered with the rest of the Israelites.
The Arrangement of the Tribes in Camp
34:
So the people of Israel did everything the 
LORD
 had commanded Moses. They camped, each under his own banner, and they marched, each with his own clan.
The Arrangement of the Tribes in Camp

1:
This is the family of Aaron and Moses at the time the 
LORD
 spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai.
2: Aaron had four sons: Nadab, the eldest, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
Aaron's Sons
3: They were anointed and ordained as priests,
Aaron's Sons
4:
but Nadab and Abihu were killed when they offered unholy fire to the 
LORD
 in the Sinai Desert. They had no children, so Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests during Aaron's lifetime.
The Levites are Appointed to Serve the Priests
5:
The 
LORD
 said to Moses,
The Levites are Appointed to Serve the Priests
6: “Bring forward the tribe of Levi and appoint them as servants of Aaron the priest.
The Levites are Appointed to Serve the Priests
7: They shall do the work required for the Tent of my presence and perform duties for the priests and for the whole community.
The Levites are Appointed to Serve the Priests
8: They shall take charge of all the equipment of the Tent and perform the duties for the rest of the Israelites.
The Levites are Appointed to Serve the Priests
9: The only responsibility the Levites have is to serve Aaron and his sons.
The Levites are Appointed to Serve the Priests
10: You shall appoint Aaron and his sons to carry out the duties of the priesthood; anyone else who tries to do so shall be put to death.”
The Levites are Appointed to Serve the Priests
11:
The 
LORD
 said to Moses,
The Levites are Appointed to Serve the Priests
12-13:
“The Levites are now to be mine. When I killed all the firstborn of the Egyptians, I consecrated as my own the eldest son of each Israelite family and the firstborn of every animal. Now, instead of having the firstborn sons of Israel as my own, I have the Levites; they will belong to me. I am the 
LORD
.”
14:
In the Sinai Desert the 
LORD
 commanded Moses
The Census of the Levites
15: to register the Levites by clans and families, enrolling every male a month old or older,
The Census of the Levites
16: and Moses did so.
The Census of the Levites
17-20: Levi had three sons: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari, who were the ancestors of the clans that bear their names. Gershon had two sons: Libni and Shimei; Kohath had four sons: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel; and Merari had two sons: Mahli and Mushi. They were the ancestors of the families that bear their names.
21: The clan of Gershon was composed of the families of Libni and Shimei.
The Census of the Levites
22: The total number of males one month old or older that were enrolled was 7,500.
The Census of the Levites
23: This clan was to camp on the west behind the Tent,
The Census of the Levites
24: with Eliasaph son of Lael as chief of the clan.
The Census of the Levites
25: They were responsible for the Tent, its inner cover, its outer cover, the curtain for the entrance,
The Census of the Levites
26: the curtains for the court which is round the Tent and the altar, and the curtain for the entrance of the court. They were responsible for all the service connected with these items.
The Census of the Levites
27: The clan of Kohath was composed of the families of Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
The Census of the Levites
28: The total number of males one month old or older that were enrolled was 8,600.
The Census of the Levites
29: This clan was to camp on the south side of the Tent,
The Census of the Levites
30: with Elizaphan son of Uzziel as chief of the clan.
The Census of the Levites
31: They were responsible for the Covenant Box, the table, the lampstand, the altars, the utensils the priests use in the Holy Place, and the curtain at the entrance to the Most Holy Place. They were responsible for all the service connected with these items.
The Census of the Levites
32: The chief of the Levites was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest. He was in charge of those who carried out the duties in the Holy Place.
The Census of the Levites
33: The clan of Merari was composed of the families of Mahli and Mushi.
The Census of the Levites
34: The total number of males one month old or older that were enrolled was 6,200.
The Census of the Levites
35: This clan was to camp on the north side of the Tent, with Zuriel son of Abihail as chief of the clan.
The Census of the Levites
36: They were assigned responsibility for the frames for the Tent, its bars, posts, bases, and all its fittings. They were responsible for all the service connected with these items.
37: They were also responsible for the posts, bases, pegs, and ropes for the outer court.
38: Moses and Aaron and his sons were to camp in front of the Tent on the east. They were responsible for carrying out the services performed in the Holy Place for the people of Israel. Anyone else who tried to do so was to be put to death.
39:
The total number of all the Levite males one month old or older that Moses enrolled by clans at the command of the 
LORD
, was 22,000.
The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
40-41:
The 
LORD
 said to Moses, “All of Israel's firstborn sons belong to me. So register by name every firstborn male Israelite, one month old or older. But in place of them I claim all the Levites as mine! I am the 
LORD
! I also claim the livestock of the Levites in place of all the firstborn of the livestock.”
The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
42: Moses obeyed, and registered all the firstborn males
The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
43: one month old or older; the total was 22,273.
The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
44: The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
45: “Now dedicate the Levites as mine in place of all the firstborn Israelite sons, and dedicate the livestock of the Levites in place of the firstborn of the Israelites' livestock.
The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
46: Since the firstborn Israelite sons outnumber the Levites by 273, you must buy back the extra sons.
The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
47: For each one pay five pieces of silver, according to the official standard,
The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
48: and give this money to Aaron and his sons.”
The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
49: The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
50: The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons
51: and gave them to Aaron and his sons.
The Levites Take the Place of the Firstborn Sons

1: The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
2: to take a census of the Levite clan of Kohath by sub-clans and families,
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
3:
and to register all the men between the ages of thirty and fifty who were qualified to work in the Tent of the 
LORD
's presence.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
4: Their service involves the most holy things.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
5:
The 
LORD
 gave Moses the following instructions. When it is time to break camp, Aaron and his sons shall enter the Tent, take down the curtain in front of the Covenant Box, and cover the Box with it.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
6: They shall put a fine leather cover over it, spread a blue cloth on top, and then insert the carrying poles.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
7:
They shall spread a blue cloth over the table for the bread offered to the 
LORD
 and put on it the dishes, the incense bowls, the offering bowls, and the jars for the wine offering. There shall always be bread on the table.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
8: They shall spread a red cloth over all this, put a fine leather cover over it, and then insert the carrying poles.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
9: They shall take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand, with its lamps, tongs, trays, and all the olive oil containers.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
10: They shall wrap it and all its equipment in a fine leather cover and place it on a carrying frame.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
11: Next they shall spread a blue cloth over the gold altar, put a fine leather cover over it, and then insert the carrying poles.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
12: They shall take all the utensils used in the Holy Place, wrap them in a blue cloth, put a fine leather cover over them, and place them on a carrying frame.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
13: They shall remove the greasy ashes from the altar and spread a purple cloth over it.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
14: They shall put on it all the equipment used in the service at the altar: firepans, hooks, shovels, and basins. Then they shall put a fine leather cover over it and insert the carrying poles.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
15:
When it is time to break camp, the clan of Kohath shall come to carry the sacred objects only after Aaron and his sons have finished covering them and all their equipment. The clan of Kohath must not touch the sacred objects, or they will die.
These are the responsibilities of the clan of Kohath whenever the Tent is moved.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
16:
Eleazar son of Aaron the priest shall be responsible for the whole Tent and for the oil for the lamps, the incense, the grain offerings, the anointing oil, and everything else in the Tent that has been consecrated to the 
LORD
.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
17:
The 
LORD
 said to Moses and Aaron,
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
18: “Do not let the clan of Kohath
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
19: be killed by coming near these most sacred objects. To prevent this happening, Aaron and his sons shall go in and assign each man his task and tell him what to carry.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Kohath
20:
But if the Kohathites enter the Tent and see the priests preparing the sacred objects for moving,
 they will die.”
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Gershon
4.20 see… moving; or see the sacred objects even for a moment.
21:
The 
LORD
 told Moses
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Gershon
22: to take a census of the Levite clan of Gershon by sub-clans and families,
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Gershon
23:
and to register all the men between the ages of thirty and fifty who were qualified to work in the Tent of the 
LORD
's presence.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Gershon
24: They shall be responsible for carrying the following objects:
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Gershon
25: the Tent, its inner cover, its outer cover, the fine leather cover on top of it, the curtain for the entrance,
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Gershon
26: the curtains and ropes for the court that is round the Tent and the altar, the curtains for the entrance of the court, and all the fittings used in setting up these objects. They shall perform all the tasks required for these things.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Gershon
27: Moses and Aaron shall see to it that the Gershonites perform all the duties and carry everything that Aaron and his sons assign to them.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Gershon
28: These are the responsibilities of the Gershon clan in the Tent; they shall carry them out under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Merari
29:
The 
LORD
 told Moses to take a census of the Levite clan of Merari by sub-clans and families,
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Merari
30:
and to register all the men between the ages of thirty and fifty who were qualified to work in the Tent of the 
LORD
's presence.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Merari
31: They shall be responsible for carrying the frames, bars, posts, and bases of the Tent,
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Merari
32: and the posts, bases, pegs, and ropes of the court round the Tent, with all the fittings used in setting them up. Each man will be responsible for carrying specific items.
The Duties of the Levite Clan of Merari
33: These are the responsibilities of the Merari clan in their service in the Tent: they shall carry them out under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
The Census of the Levites
34-48:
Following the LORD 's command, Moses, Aaron, and the leaders of the community took a census of the three Levite clans, Kohath, Gershon, and Merari. They did this by sub-clans and families and registered all the men between the ages of thirty and fifty who were qualified to work in the Tent of the LORD 's presence, as follows:
ClanNumber
Kohath2,750
Gershon2,630
Merari3,200
Total:8,580
49:
Each man was registered as the 
LORD
 had commanded Moses; and at the command of the 
LORD
 given through Moses, each man was assigned responsibility for his task of serving or carrying.

2: “Command the people of Israel to expel from the camp everyone with a dreaded skin disease or a bodily discharge and everyone who is unclean by contact with a corpse.
Unclean People
3: Send all these ritually unclean people out, so that they will not defile the camp, where I live among my people.”
Unclean People
4: The Israelites obeyed and expelled them all from the camp.
Repayment for Wrongs Done
5:
The 
LORD
 gave Moses
Repayment for Wrongs Done
6:
the following instructions for the people of Israel. When anyone is unfaithful to the 
LORD
 and commits a wrong against someone,
Repayment for Wrongs Done
7: he must confess his sin and make full repayment, plus an additional twenty per cent, to the person he has wronged.
Repayment for Wrongs Done
8:
But if that person has died and has no near relative to whom payment can be made, it shall be given to the 
LORD
 for the priest. This payment is in addition to the ram used to perform the ritual of purification for the guilty person.
Repayment for Wrongs Done
9:
Also every special contribution which the Israelites offer to the 
LORD
 belongs to the priest to whom they present it.
Repayment for Wrongs Done
10: Each priest shall keep the offerings presented to him.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
11:
The 
LORD
 commanded Moses
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
12-14: to give the Israelites the following instructions. It may happen that a man becomes suspicious that his wife is unfaithful to him and has defiled herself by having intercourse with another man. But the husband may not be certain, for his wife may have kept it secret — there was no witness, and she was not caught in the act. Or it may happen that a husband becomes suspicious of his wife, even though she has not been unfaithful.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
15: In either case the man shall take his wife to the priest. He shall also take the required offering of one kilogramme of barley flour, but he shall not pour any olive oil on it or put any incense on it, because it is an offering from a suspicious husband, made to bring the truth to light.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
16: The priest shall bring the woman forward and make her stand in front of the altar.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
17:
He shall pour some holy water into a clay bowl and take some of the earth that is on the floor of the Tent of the 
LORD
's presence and put it in the water to make it bitter.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
18: Then he shall loosen the woman's hair and put the offering of flour in her hands. In his hands the priest shall hold the bowl containing the bitter water that brings a curse.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
19: Then the priest shall make the woman agree to this oath spoken by the priest: “If you have not committed adultery, you will not be harmed by the curse that this water brings.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
20: But if you have committed adultery,
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
21:
may the 
LORD
 make your name a curse among your people. May he cause your genital organs to shrink and your stomach to swell up.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
22:
May this water enter your stomach and cause it to swell up and your genital organs to shrink.”
The woman shall respond, “I agree; may the 
LORD
 do so.”
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
23: Then the priest shall write this curse down and wash the writing off into the bowl of bitter water.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
24: Before he makes the woman drink the water, which may then cause her bitter pain,
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
25:
the priest shall take the offering of flour out of the woman's hands, hold it out in dedication to the 
LORD
, and present it on the altar.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
26: Then he shall take a handful of it as a token offering and burn it on the altar. Finally, he shall make the woman drink the water.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
27: If she has committed adultery, the water will cause bitter pain; her stomach will swell up and her genital organs will shrink. Her name will become a curse among her people.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
28: But if she is innocent, she will not be harmed and will be able to bear children.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
29-30: This is the law in cases where a man is jealous and becomes suspicious that his wife has committed adultery. The woman shall be made to stand in front of the altar, and the priest shall perform this ritual.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands
31: The husband shall be free of guilt, but the woman, if guilty, must suffer the consequences.
Cases of Wives with Suspicious Husbands

2:
to give the following instructions to the people of Israel. Any men or women who make a special vow to become Nazirites and dedicate themselves to the 
LORD
Rules for Nazirites
3: shall abstain from wine and beer. They shall not drink any kind of drink made from grapes or eat any grapes or raisins.
Rules for Nazirites
4: As long as they are Nazirites, they shall not eat anything that comes from a grapevine, not even the seeds or skins of grapes.
Rules for Nazirites
5:
As long as they are under the Nazirite vow, they must not cut their hair or shave. They are bound by the vow for the full time that they are dedicated to the 
LORD
, and they shall let their hair grow.
Rules for Nazirites
6-7: Their hair is the sign of their dedication to God, and so they must not defile themselves by going near a corpse, not even that of their father, mother, brother, or sister.
8:
As long as they are Nazirites, they are consecrated to the 
LORD
.
Rules for Nazirites
9: If the consecrated hair of a Nazirite is defiled because he or she is right beside someone who suddenly dies, they must wait seven days and then shave off their hair; and so they become ritually clean.
Rules for Nazirites
10:
On the eighth day they shall bring two doves or two pigeons to the priest at the entrance of the Tent of the 
LORD
's presence.
Rules for Nazirites
11: The priest shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, to perform the ritual of purification for them because of their contact with a corpse. On the same day they shall reconsecrate their hair
Rules for Nazirites
12:
and rededicate to the 
LORD
 their time as Nazirites. The previous period of time doesn't count, because their consecrated hair was defiled. As a repayment offering they shall bring a one-year-old lamb.
Rules for Nazirites
13: When Nazirites complete their vows, they shall perform this ritual. They shall go to the entrance of the Tent
Rules for Nazirites
14:
and present to the 
LORD
 three animals without any defects: a one-year-old male lamb for a burnt offering, a one-year-old ewe lamb for a sin offering, and a ram for a fellowship offering.
Rules for Nazirites
15: They shall also offer a basket of bread made without yeast: thick loaves made of flour mixed with olive oil and biscuits brushed with olive oil, and in addition the required offerings of corn and wine.
Rules for Nazirites
16:
The priest shall present all these to the 
LORD
 and offer the sin offering and the burnt offering.
Rules for Nazirites
17:
He shall sacrifice the ram to the 
LORD
 as a fellowship offering, and offer it with the basket of bread; he shall also present the offerings of corn and wine.
Rules for Nazirites
18: At the entrance of the Tent the Nazirite shall shave off his or her hair and put it on the fire on which the fellowship offering is being burnt.
Rules for Nazirites
19: Then, when the shoulder of the ram is boiled, the priest shall take it and put it, together with one thick loaf of bread and one biscuit from the basket, into the hands of the Nazirite.
Rules for Nazirites
20:
Next, the priest shall present them as a special gift to the 
LORD
; they are a sacred offering for the priest, in addition to the breast and the leg of the ram which by law belong to the priest. After that, the Nazirite may drink wine.
Rules for Nazirites
21: These are the regulations for Nazirites; but if a Nazirite promises an offering beyond what his or her vow requires them to give, they must fulfil exactly the promise they made.
The Priestly Blessing
22:
The 
LORD
 commanded Moses
The Priestly Blessing
23: to tell Aaron and his sons to use the following words in blessing the people of Israel:
The Priestly Blessing
24: The Priestly Blessing
25: The Priestly Blessing
26:
May the 
LORD
 look on you with favour and give you peace.
The Priestly Blessing
27:
And the 
LORD
 said, “If they pronounce my name as a blessing upon the people of Israel, I will bless them.”
The Priestly Blessing

1:
On the day Moses finished setting up the Tent of the 
LORD
's presence, he anointed and dedicated the Tent and all its equipment, and the altar and all its equipment.
2: Then the clan chiefs who were leaders in the tribes of Israel, the same men who were in charge of the census,
The Offerings of the Leaders
3:
brought their offerings to the 
LORD
: six wagons and twelve oxen, a wagon for every two leaders and an ox for each leader. After they had presented them,
The Offerings of the Leaders
4:
the 
LORD
 said to Moses,
The Offerings of the Leaders
5: “Accept these gifts for use in the work to be done for the Tent; give them to the Levites according to the work they have to do.”
The Offerings of the Leaders
6: So Moses gave the wagons and the oxen to the Levites.
The Offerings of the Leaders
7: He gave two wagons and four oxen to the Gershonites,
The Offerings of the Leaders
8: and four wagons and eight oxen to the Merarites. All their work was to be done under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron.
The Offerings of the Leaders
9: But Moses gave no wagons or oxen to the Kohathites, because the sacred objects they took care of had to be carried on their shoulders.
The Offerings of the Leaders
10: The leaders also brought offerings to celebrate the dedication of the altar. When they were ready to present their gifts at the altar,
The Offerings of the Leaders
11:
the 
LORD
 said to Moses, “Tell them that each day for a period of twelve days one of the leaders is to present his gifts for the dedication of the altar.”
The Offerings of the Leaders
12-83:
They presented their offerings in the following order:
DayTribeLeader
1stJudahNahshon son of Amminadab
2ndIssacharNethanel son of Zuar
3rdZebulunEliab son of Helon
4thReubenElizur son of Shedeur
5thSimeonShelumiel son of Zurishaddai
6thGadEliasaph son of Deuel

56: If you do not drive them out, I will destroy you, as I planned to destroy them.”

1:
In the plains of Moab across the Jordan from Jericho the 
LORD
 said to Moses,
The Cities Assigned to the Levites
2: “Tell the Israelites that from the property they receive they must give the Levites some cities to live in and pasture land round the cities.
The Cities Assigned to the Levites
3: These cities will belong to the Levites, and they will live there. The pasture land will be for their cattle and all their other animals.
The Cities Assigned to the Levites
4: The pasture land is to extend outwards from the city walls 450 metres in each direction,
The Cities Assigned to the Levites
5: so that there is a square area measuring 900 metres on each side, with the city in the middle.
The Cities Assigned to the Levites
6: You are to give the Levites six cities of refuge to which a man can escape if he kills someone accidentally. In addition, give them 42 other cities
The Cities Assigned to the Levites
7: with their pasture land, making a total of 48.
The Cities Assigned to the Levites
8: The number of Levite cities in each tribe is to be determined according to the size of its territory.”
The Cities of Refuge
9:
The 
LORD
 told Moses
The Cities of Refuge
10: to say to the people of Israel: “When you cross the River Jordan and enter the land of Canaan,
The Cities of Refuge
11: you are to choose cities of refuge to which a man can escape if he kills someone accidentally.
The Cities of Refuge
12: There he will be safe from the dead person's relative who seeks revenge. No one accused of manslaughter is to be put to death without a public trial.
The Cities of Refuge
13: Choose six cities,
The Cities of Refuge
14: three east of the Jordan and three in the land of Canaan.
The Cities of Refuge
15: These will serve as cities of refuge for Israelites and for foreigners who are temporary or permanent residents. Anyone who kills someone accidentally can escape to one of them.
The Cities of Refuge
16-18: “If, however, anyone uses a weapon of iron or stone or wood to kill someone, he is guilty of murder and is to be put to death.
19: The dead person's nearest relative has the responsibility for putting the murderer to death. When he finds him, he is to kill him.
The Cities of Refuge
20: “If anyone hates someone and kills him by pushing him down or by throwing something at him
The Cities of Refuge
21: or by striking him with his fist, he is guilty of murder and is to be put to death. The dead man's nearest relative has the responsibility for putting the murderer to death. When he finds him, he is to kill him.
The Cities of Refuge
22: “But suppose anyone accidentally kills someone he does not hate, whether by pushing him down or by throwing something at him.
The Cities of Refuge
23: Or suppose that, without looking, anyone throws a stone that kills someone whom he did not intend to hurt and who was not his enemy.
The Cities of Refuge
24: In such cases the community shall judge in favour of the one who caused the death and not in favour of the dead person's relative who is seeking revenge.
The Cities of Refuge
25: The community is to rescue the one guilty of manslaughter from the dead person's relative, and they are to return him to the city of refuge to which he had escaped. He must live there until the death of the man who is then High Priest.
The Cities of Refuge
26: If the one guilty of manslaughter leaves the city of refuge to which he has escaped
The Cities of Refuge
27: and if the dead person's relative finds him and kills him, this act of revenge is not murder.
The Cities of Refuge
28: The person guilty of manslaughter must remain in the city of refuge until the death of the High Priest, but after that he may return home.
The Cities of Refuge
29: These rules apply to you and your descendants wherever you may live.
The Cities of Refuge
30: “Anyone accused of murder may be found guilty and put to death only on the evidence of two or more witnesses; the evidence of one witness is not sufficient to support an accusation of murder.
The Cities of Refuge
31: A murderer must be put to death. He cannot escape this penalty by the payment of money.
The Cities of Refuge
32: If someone has fled to a city of refuge, do not allow him to make a payment in order to return home before the death of the High Priest.
The Cities of Refuge
33: If you did this, you would defile the land where you are living. Murder defiles the land, and except by the death of the murderer there is no way to perform the ritual of purification for the land where someone has been murdered.
The Cities of Refuge
34:
Do not defile the land where you are living, because I am the 
LORD
 and I live among the people of Israel.”
